zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Drew於1738年10月13日出生在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德多福時，他的父親Clement Drew Sr44歲，母親Mary Bunker40歲。他於1758年與Lovey Bunker結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1803年8月16日在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德巴恩斯亞德享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Samuel Drew于1738年10月13日出生在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德多福时，他的父亲Clement Drew Sr44岁，母亲Mary Bunker40岁。他于1758年与Lovey Bunker结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1803年8月16日在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德巴恩斯亞德享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Drew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a Norman French personal name Dreus based on ancient Germanic Drogo, the name of one of the sons of Charlemagne (compare Gothic driugan ‘to serve as a soldier’). In Old French this name became Dreus, Drues (nominative) and Dru, Driu, Dreu (accusative) and was introduced to England by the Normans. For further information about this name, see also Montague .
English: from the personal name Drew, a pet form of Andrew which was confused with the same name of Old French origin (see 1 above).

Irish: when not an adoption of the English surname, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Druaidh or Ó Druaidh or Ó Draoi ‘son’ and ‘descendant of the druid’, from draoi ‘druid’, genitive druadh or draoi. All of these were often Anglicized as Drury .

variants.name: Drews
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerhard, Gunter, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Otto, Reinhold, Bernd, Detlef, Egon, Erna.
English: variant of Drew with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also found in Guernsey in the Channel Islands.
Americanized form of German, Danish, and Dutch   Drewes  .

variants.name: Drewer
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Droart, Drouart (which is probably from ancient Germanic Droghard, with the first element as in Drew + hard ‘brave, hardy’).

variants.name: Drewett
meanings: English (Wiltshire, Berkshire, and Surrey): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Druet, a diminutive of Drue, Dreu (from ancient Germanic Drogo); see Drew 1. Alternatively, the name may be from a diminutive of Old French dru ‘lover’; see Drew 4.

variants.name: Dronet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Desire, Olympe.

French (mainly Loire-Atlantique): from a diminutive of the personal name Dron, derived from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ).

variants.name: Droz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Aida, Cruz Maria, Diego, Jose, Miguel, Milagros, Nydia, Osvaldo, Rodriguez, Vidal.

French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). This surname is also found in Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Drea
meanings: Irish (Kilkenny): shortened Anglicized form of Ó Draoi (see Drew ).

variants.name: Montague
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

variants.name: Drouin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Benoit, Jacques, Andre, Clermont, Emile, Gaston, Gilles, Ovide, Pierre, Serge.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Droue, a variant of Dreue, which is from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). Compare Derouen , Derouin , and Tron .

variants.name: Drury
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Drury  .

variants.name: Drouhard
meanings: French:
from the ancient Germanic personal name Droghard, composed of the elements drog (presumably meaning ‘battle’; see also Drew ) and hard ‘hard, strong’.
alternatively, a derivative of the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names like Droghard (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: The Bill of Rights
historicEvents.description: After the Constitution was made, some objections were raised by Anti-Federalists. So, in response ten amendments were discussed and voted on to become The Bill of Rights. These rights were made to clarify and guarantee certain freedoms of residents of the country.

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Drew was born on 13 October 1738, in Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States, his father, Clement Drew Sr, was 44 and his mother, Mary Bunker, was 40. He married Lovey Bunker in 1758. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 August 1803, in Barnstead, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 64.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Drew è nato il il 13 ottobre 1738 nel Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Clement Drew Sr, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Mary Bunker, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Lovey Bunker nel 1758. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 agosto 1803, nel Barnstead, Strafford, New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Samuel Drewが1738年10月13日にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードドバーで生まれた時，父Clement Drew Srは44歳，母Mary Bunkerは40歳であった。1758年にLovey Bunkerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1803年8月16日にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードバーンステアドで64歳で死去した。

ko.Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버
ko.Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial A: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 도버
ko.Madbury, Strafford, New Hampshire, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 매드버이
ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Drew(이)가 1738년 10월 13일 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 도버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Clement Drew Sr(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Bunker(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1758년 Lovey Bunker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1803년 8월 16일 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 바른스테아드에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Drew nasceu em 13 de outubro de 1738, em Dover, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Clement Drew Sr, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Mary Bunker, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Lovey Bunker em 1758. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 16 de agosto de 1803, em Barnstead, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, com 64 anos.

ru.Madbury, Strafford, New Hampshire, Briti: мадбури, страффорд, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Drew родился 13 октября 1738 г. в местности довир, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Clement Drew Sr было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Bunker, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Lovey Bunker в 1758 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 августа 1803 г. в местности барнстид, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Samuel Drew nació el 13 de octubre de 1738, en Dover, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos, su papá, Clement Drew Sr, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Mary Bunker, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Lovey Bunker en 1758. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de agosto de 1803, en Barnstead, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 64 años.
